title: achras zapota var. lobata
intro: Achras zapota var. lobata, also known as the Chicozapote, is a variety of the sapodilla tree, native to Central and South America, characterized by its small size and unique fruit. The medicinal properties of the plant are attributed to its high concentration of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Chicozapote include its ability to aid in digestion, lower cholesterol levels, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Traditionally, the plant's roots and bark are used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions.
